name: Los Oteros, Reliegos y Mansilla de las Mulas – circular from Palanquinos
name_original: Los Oteros, Reliegos y Mansilla de las Mulas – circular desde Palanquinos
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Villanueva De Las Manzanas are characterized by accessible, generally flat or gently rolling terrain, making the area suitable for various skill levels. The region, located in the province of León, Spain, features extensive agricultural landscapes that offer open views and a tranquil environment. Routes often follow rural roads and paths, sometimes alongside the Esla River, providing picturesque scenery. This topography is ideal for cyclists seeking pleasant rides through the Spanish countryside. Best touring cycling routes around Villanueva De Las Manzanas The most popular touring cycling route is Los Oteros, Reliegos y Mansilla de las Mulas – circular from Palanquinos, a 32.2 miles (51.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the rural surroundings, passing through notable local villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is León Cathedral – Plaza Mayor loop from Mansilla Mayor, a moderate 28.5 miles (45.9 km) path. This route offers a journey from Mansilla Mayor towards the city of León, combining rural stretches with urban approaches. Local touring cyclists also love the Bercianos del Real Camino – Rest Area at Ermita de Perales loop from Palanquinos, a 101.7 miles (163.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 9 hours 43 minutes. This difficult route provides a longer, more challenging experience across the region. Touring cycling around Villanueva De Las Manzanas is defined by agricultural lands, the Esla River, and rural roads, offering a blend of natural and cultural exploration. The network provides options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Villanueva De Las Manzanas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from a few reviews. More than 80 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Las Manzanas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Villanueva de las Manzanas?
A: There are 9 touring cycling routes available around Villanueva de las Manzanas. These routes offer a range of experiences, from easy rides through agricultural landscapes to more challenging journeys across the region.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for touring cycling in this area?
A: The terrain around Villanueva de las Manzanas is generally flat or gently rolling, characterized by extensive agricultural lands. Routes often follow rural roads and paths, sometimes alongside the Esla River, providing picturesque and tranquil scenery ideal for touring cyclists of various skill levels.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, there are 2 easy touring cycling routes available, making the area suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. The generally flat topography of the region is very accommodating for less experienced cyclists.

Q: Can I find longer or more challenging touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a greater challenge, there are 2 difficult touring cycling routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40303036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bercianos del Real Camino – Rest Area at Ermita de Perales loop from Palanquinos&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 100 miles and offers a longer, more demanding experience through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032771691/los-oteros-reliegos-y-mansilla-de-las-mulas-circular-from-palanquinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Oteros, Reliegos y Mansilla de las Mulas – circular from Palanquinos&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 32-mile route exploring rural surroundings and local villages.

Q: What kind of attractions or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes offer a blend of natural and cultural exploration. You can encounter historical bridges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esla River Bridge, Mansilla de las Mulas&lt;/a&gt;, or unique natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6771999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reliegos Meteorite Impact Site&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass through traditional Leonese villages, offering glimpses into local heritage.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families?
A: Given the generally flat or gently rolling terrain and the presence of easy routes, many of the touring cycling paths around Villanueva de las Manzanas are suitable for families. These routes often follow quiet rural roads, providing a safe and enjoyable environment for all ages.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Villanueva de las Manzanas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Villanueva de las Manzanas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from several reviews. Cyclists often praise the tranquil environment, the accessible terrain, and the opportunity to explore the Spanish countryside and local villages.

Q: Can I cycle along the Esla River?
A: Yes, the Esla River is a prominent feature of the landscape, and several routes incorporate sections along or across it. The river and its floodplains provide picturesque and relatively flat paths, enhancing the scenic beauty of your ride. You might even cross the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esla River Bridge, Mansilla de las Mulas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there specific points of interest related to the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, the broader Castilla y León region is known for its extensive network of routes, including those that intersect with the Camino de Santiago. You might encounter landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6756922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago Distance Marker: 334.9 km to León&lt;/a&gt;, offering a connection to this historic pilgrimage route.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for touring cycling routes here?
A: The routes vary significantly in length and duration. You can find moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31555568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León Cathedral – Plaza Mayor loop from Mansilla Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 28.5 miles (45.9 km) and takes around 3 hours. Longer, more challenging routes can extend over 100 miles and take upwards of 9 hours to complete.

Q: Are there routes that pass through local villages or offer cultural insights?
A: Many routes are designed to showcase the local heritage. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032771691/los-oteros-reliegos-y-mansilla-de-las-mulas-circular-from-palanquinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Oteros, Reliegos y Mansilla de las Mulas – circular from Palanquinos&lt;/a&gt; route explores rural surroundings and passes through notable local villages, allowing cyclists to experience traditional Leonese life and visit landmarks like the Church of San Juan Degollado.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Villanueva de las Manzanas?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. These seasons provide comfortable weather for exploring the agricultural landscapes and rural roads without the intense heat of summer or the chill of winter.
tips: This small town, which shares a municipality with the neighboring Gigosos de los Oteros, is a quiet and pleasant place where you can perhaps make a different stop and try some wine or pomace in a private winery, as long as you don't have much more to pedal. ..

The wild and colorful facade of this bar in Reliegos, designed with graffiti, invites you to leave your own personal message on the Camino. If you have nothing on your mind, you can easily make yourself comfortable on one of the chairs with a refreshing beer and read through the texts of his predecessors.

At this point, the LE-6615 provincial road, which is part of the Camino de Santiago and connects Reliegos with Mansilla de las Mulas, crosses the A-60 motorway via an elevated viaduct, which serves as a good observation point on an eminently flat route. This highway is only a small section of the projected connection between León and Valladolid, a stalled project with local claims for being finished to unite the two capitals but which also brings with it environmental and patrimonial impact concerns, as well as those who question its true need, existing and a highway connection between the two cities, although not so directly.
